definitions: A type of formation that hangs from the ceiling of caves, hot springs, or man-made structures such as bridges and mines. It is a type of speleothem.
A secondary mineral deposit, typically conical or cylindrical in shape, that is formed on the roof of a cave by the dripping of mineralized water.
examples: The cave was adorned with numerous stalactites.
Water dripped from the tips of the stalactites.
We observed the growth of stalactites over many years.
